case_name: SUSAN OAKES v. MARK A. FOX, M.D.

summary.brief: SUSAN OAKES v. MARK A. FOX, M.D.. Medical malpractice case involving oncology.

evidence_index.text: In 2018, plaintiff Susan Oakes was diagnosed with early-stage breast cancer after a mammogram and a biopsy revealed a small invasive ductal carcinoma.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ms. Oakes, age 58 at the time, thereafter, met with Dr. Mark A. Fox, who maintains that he offered Ms. Oakes the options of: (a) lumpectomy, radiation, and sentinel lymph node biopsy (“SLNB”); or (b) mastectomy and axillary lymph node dissection (“ALND”).
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Steven Efird, Plaintiffs’ expert, acknowledged in a deposition that, based upon his review of the record in the matter, he found Dr. Fox’s charting to be truthful that Ms. Oakes refused radiation.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Steven Efird

evidence_index.text: Ms. Oakes asserts the surgery by Dr. Fox resulted in the unnecessary removal of her breasts and caused incurable lymphedema and pain, swelling, weakness, and decreased range of motion in her arm and shoulder.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court granted summary judgment to the defendants. We affirm.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

evidence_index.text: The court found that Plaintiffs’ “response to the defendants’ motion for summary judgment was not timely filed and will not be considered.”
evidence_index.speaker: Trial court

evidence_index.text: Dr. Efird admitted that “the way in which Dr. Fox [. . .] charted the treatment options did not cause any injury to Ms. Oakes” and that the charting “did not cause any secondary provider to take a different or adverse path in treatment.”
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Steven Efird

evidence_index.text: Defendants have put forth sufficient evidence to affirmatively negate the essential elements of Plaintiffs’ claim of medical negligence arising from Dr. Fox’s performance of the double mastectomy and ALND.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

evidence_index.text: On the day of the surgery, Ms. Oakes signed a consent to procedure form which reveals she expressly consented to the ALND procedure without any limitation on the number of lymph nodes to be removed.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Court having found that (1) Dr. Fox properly and sufficiently explained all possible surgical options for Ms. Oakes’ breast cancer, the likely outcomes and risks of each—including lymphedema—and (2) after these discussions, Ms. Oakes elected to proceed with a double mastectomy and axillary lymph node dissection (“ALND”),
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: In 2018, Susan Oakes was diagnosed with early-stage breast cancer after a mammogram and biopsy revealed a small invasive ductal carcinoma.

requestables.name: Surgical Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with Dr. Mark A. Fox regarding surgical options for breast cancer treatment.

reveal.result_summary: Options discussed: lumpectomy with radiation and SLNB, or mastectomy with ALND.
reveal.clinical_significance: Informed consent and treatment options discussion.

timeline_phases.name: Initial Diagnosis
timeline_phases.description: Patient diagnosed with early-stage breast cancer.

timeline_phases.name: Surgical Decision
timeline_phases.description: Patient decides on surgical treatment after consultation.

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient deciding on surgical treatment for breast cancer.
decision_points.prompt: What surgical treatment should be recommended?

options.description: Recommend lumpectomy with radiation and SLNB.
options.clinical_reasoning: Less invasive, preserves breast, similar survival rates.
More invasive, higher risk of lymphedema, unnecessary for early-stage cancer.

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant recommended double mastectomy with ALND.
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Patient was informed of options and chose mastectomy.

expected_action_court.description: Lumpectomy with radiation and SLNB should have been recommended.
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated similar survival rates with less invasive procedure.

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: The court found that the recommendation for mastectomy was not necessary given the early-stage diagnosis.
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Patient should have been advised on the benefits of breast-conserving surgery.
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony.

patient_outcome.description: Patient underwent double mastectomy and developed lymphedema.
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Chronic lymphedema, pain, and decreased range of motion.

key_findings: No breach of standard of care found.

factual_timeline.event: Patient diagnosed with early-stage breast cancer

court_findings.finding: No breach of standard of care
